paper_title: Building a Large Annotated Corpus of English: The Penn Treebank
evaluation_method: Language modeling evaluation on the Penn Treebank corpus. The dataset consists of 929k training words, 73k validation words, and 82k test words from Wall Street Journal articles. Standard preprocessing: words lowercased, numbers replaced with N, newlines replaced with <eos>, punctuation removed, vocabulary restricted to most frequent 10k words with rest replaced by <unk>. Models are evaluated by computing perplexity (exponential of the average per-word negative log-likelihood) on the test set. Lower perplexity indicates better language modeling.
